author: @tehspamgozehere
text:
Sadly, no. Bush pig, most likely. Or something along those lines. The "spikes" are leaves and foliage, which are also depicted in every other carving. A stego has a thagomiser on a long tail. That carving has a short unadorned tail. It's also lacking the very prominent three horns. You could argue it might be a juvenile, but it'd be very odd to put a single juvenile in a relief alongside... A lionmonkeyman at the bottom, and a bear-centaur-woman at the top?

It's a beautiful piece of work, and fascinating for it's own sake. But the recent upsurge in science denialism and religious fundamentalism, extremism and literalism has taken this as "proof" that literally all of science is wrong.. And they never look at the rest of the carving. As you can likely tell from the other comments on this video alone.

I'm envious of you for being able to go and see it in person though.

author: @JoelTConnors
text: This is fascinating. I think it's possible that the Cambodians found fossils of Stegosaurids and made carvings of them. Or possibly it's a sheer coincidence that it looks like a Stegosaurus.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's pretty wild isn't it - that was the same temple they filmed lara croft in.

replies.author: @teetouch4764
replies.text: +Joel Connors The ancient Khmers probably found fossils of a Stegosaurus and then piece them together and carved the picture out. It's quite possible that these people can do this a thousand years ago which proves how advance they were.

replies.author: @buakawfan333
replies.text: If they had the ability to understand comparative anatomy and paleontology  to that degree, that's a marvel in itself. Think about how much science goes into this even today, with teams of experts all putting these pieces together. Otherwise they would have had to have found a whole, very well preserved stegosaurus fossil to be able to just draw a picture like that. Not impossible circumstances but extreeeemely lucky.  But then you look at the murals of extinct elephants with 4 tusks and extinct hyenas with long tails. It adds a whole other level of weirdness to this temple and this civilization.

replies.author: @buakawfan333
replies.text: There is also the possibility that a direct descendant of the stegosaurus lived in East Asia up until 1500 years ago.....again, this is highly improbable, but not impossible. Reminds me of the stories of the "dinosaur" living in the Congo within the last few decades. If there were really only a handful of these animals left in a thick jungle and wetlands and they were very good at avoiding people, then it could be possible they would never be found by westerners. Or even if they were "stories" passed down by the natives, then they still could be referring to real animals that were still around until quite recently. The vast, and I mean vast, majority of animal fossils that are still around today are more just extreme luck than due process. Most animal carcasses are wiped clean off the record. We only see a highly distorted blip of the history in the rocks of this planet.
